observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Accountable Officers:
a) Determine and address all the reasons for the delay in the submission of the required
reports and consider imposing sanctions on the persons responsible for the delayed
submission of the financial reports and supporting documents to the COA Office. Further,
review and evaluate the performance of the finance personnel; and recommend replacement
for those found undesirable with qualified personnel with the right work attitude;
b) Conduct planning, target setting and prepare commitment reports particularly on the
timelines on the submission of transaction documents/reports and financial statements
provided in Sections 107 and 122 of PD 1445, Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006
and Sections 59 and 60, Chapter 19, Volume I of the GAM for NGAs, and Article 218 of the
Revised Penal Code to avoid repercussions stated herein; and
c) Conduct periodic monitoring of submission of required reports to COA, Department of
Budget and Management (DBM) and other regulatory bodies and see to it that all the required
reports are submitted on time.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant require all the depository banks to strictly
comply with the submission of the mandated bank statements (BS) and supporting documents
for all depository accounts maintained by the Municipality within five days after the end of
each month. In turn, within ten days from the receipts of such BS, the same should be
reconciled with the General Ledgers which shall be the basis for preparation of BRS; and
henceforth, the BRS must be regularly submitted to the Auditor within ten days after the end
of each month together with the paid checks and debit/credit memos. Likewise, prepare the
necessary journal entry voucher (JEVs) to record the reconciling items affecting the accounts.

We recommended the Management to require:
3.1.1 The Inventory Committee to conduct thorough review and physical count of the
inventories and PPE by establishing its actual conditions and whereabouts and come up with
the RPCI and RPCPPE.
3.2.1 The Inventory Committee to adhere with the submission of RPCI for the first and second
semesters not later than July 31 and January 31, respectively, and the annual RPCPPE not
later than January 31 of each year to the Audit Team for subsequent review.
3.3.1 The Supply and Property Unit and Accounting Office to strengthen its system of
maintaining its accounting and property records such as SCs, PCs, SLCs, PPELCs, RPCI and
RPCPPE to facilitate periodic reconciliation of these records to ensure reliability of the
inventories and PPE account balances as presented in the Financial Statements of the
Municipality.
3.4.1 The Municipal Accountant and Other Officers concerned should provide for the
depreciation on the depreciable assets. The Property Supply Officer should ensure that the
Property Custodian keeps and maintains updated Property Card for each PPE account to
readily provide pertinent data, thus facilitate the preparation of the Schedule of Depreciation
which shall be the basis of recording in the books of accounts the appropriate depreciation
expenses pertaining to the current year which should be charged against its appropriate
depreciation expense accounts. These are necessary in order to present fairly the PPE
accounts which its corresponding Accumulated Depreciation Expense Accounts in FS.

We recommended that the management mark all motor vehicles of the Agency with “For Official
Use Only” and to exert an effort that existing motor vehicles which are not yet government
plated be registered accordingly under which place the name of the Agency.

4.1.1 We recommended that the Management furnish the Audit Team with the copies of
MOA/Trust Agreement on all funds transferred by the NGAs to evaluate whether the funds were
utilized within the specified purpose.
4.2.1 We recommended that the Management furnish the Audit Team with the copies of
MOA/Trust Agreement on all funds transferred by the NGAs to evaluate whether the funds were
utilized within the specified purpose.

We have recommended to furnish the Auditor copy of the POs and contract documents not later
than five (5) working days from perfection and issuance of contracts together with duly
accomplished supporting documents for technical review and evaluation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the personnel concerned to submit
the list of PPAs of the Municipality, annually at the beginning of the year; and prepare the
quarterly PPA reports to include non-infrastructure projects in compliance with COA Circular
2013-004.

We recommended that Management should:
7.1. Formulate a policy on the granting of FA systematically determine the extent of aid to be
given to individuals in need. Beneficiaries should be properly screened giving priority to the
less privileged constituents.
7.2 Coordinate with the MSWO to assess request for medical and hospitalization assistance
by way of Social Case Study Reports so that only the deserving indigents can be assisted and
served.
7.3 Instruct the MSWO to require the applicants for AICS a complete submission of supporting
documents for the assistance being requested as a basis of assessment and recommendation
to make sure that only the deserving ones were provided with the necessary assistance to help
them cope during crisis situations.

We recommended that the Management should instruct the HRMO Department to submit
current plantilla of personnel every year and furnish the Audit Team copies of agency
personnel movements (i.e. approved appointment/contract of service of every newly hired,
promoted as well as the newly renewed contract) beforehand.

We recommended that Management should require the Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer
to prepare the report of properties that require insurance coverage and submit the same to
the GSIS and furnished a copy to the Audit Team for review and verification.

Further, management should ensure all insurable assets with the General Insurance Fund of
the GSIS to cover the cost of the properties in case of loss due to fire and other contingent
event in accordance with to Republic Act No. 656, otherwise known as Property Insurance
Law, as amended by PD No. 245 dated July 13, 1973.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
8.1 Require the GAD Focal Person to prepare on time the annual GPB for review and
approval by DILG and submit the approved copy and the corresponding AR to the COA Audit
Team within the prescribed period provided in COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18,
2014: and
8.2 Approve charges and disbursements on GAD Fund only if supported with DILG reviewed
and approved GPB to ascertain validity and propriety of disbursements.

Furnish a copy of the 20% DF report to the Office of the Auditor and ensure that the utilization
of the 20% DF be limited to PPAs that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures
in accordance with DBM-DILG JMC No. 2017-1 and refrain from appropriating or including
in the appropriation of development projects the personnel services, not allowable to be
charged to the 20% DF.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor requires all Revenue Collectors prepare and submit
the RAAF to the Municipal Treasurer so the latter can prepare the CRAAF and submit the
monthly reports of accountability for accountable forms in the prescribed form provided in the
Manual for NGAS for LGUs and submit the same not later than 5th day of the ensuing month
to the COA Audit Team.

We recommended that the General Manager direct the Senior Corporate Budget Analyst to
locate pertinent records and closely monitor the Construction in progress account thru the
maintenance of subsidiary records and other accounting records consistent with the provisions
of the MNGAS for LGUs. Likewise, coordinate with the Engineering Division to identify
completed projects and request submission of Property Completion and Inventory Report for
all completed projects of the Municipality of Tipo-Tipo for proper reclassification and
recording to its appropriate asset account, and provision of depreciation for depreciable
assets.

Direct the persons responsible to settle the audit suspension through compliance with the
requirements indicated in the Notice of Suspension (NS) and the persons liable for the audit
disallowance must settle the same in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated
September 15, 2009, otherwise known as the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of
Accounts and that COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Re: Revised
Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transaction must be properly
observed.

We recommended Management to strictly comply with the guidelines of the LBC, likewise full disclosure policy of the government must be adhered. We recommend further to submit a written explanation on the above audit observation.
We recommended that Management strictly adhere with the provision of RA 7160, RA 9184 and LBC 125 in the preparation and submission of the BGCM budget and supplemental APP.

We recommended management to comply with Section 3.7 of LBC 125, Section 313 of RA 7160 and Section 107 of MNGAS in taking up the Bayanihan Grant and submit to the Office of the Auditor its subsidiary ledger.

We recommended that to the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for the auditor’s immediate review and verification and to facilitate the immediate preparation of the necessary correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or errors or other reconciling items requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct balance of cash accounts in the financial statements.

a. Direct the Inventory and Disposal Committee to conduct a complete physical count inventory, annually, as required; Management should treat the matter of physical count and appraisal of the PPE as a priority concern. A separate inventory report shall be prepared for unserviceable PPE which do not contribute to the economic activity of the Municipality. Derecognize unserviceable properties in the books and facilitate disposal of these properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445.
b. Intensify efforts to maintain and prepare PPE Ledger cards (PPELC) in accordance with Section 13, Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume II which prescribes the maintenance of SL to substantiate the account.
c. The Municipal Accountant and Other Officers concerned should provide for the depreciation on the depreciable assets. The Property Supply Officer should ensure that the Property Custodian keeps and maintains updated Property Card for each PPE account to readily provide pertinent data, thus facilitate the preparation of the Schedule of Depreciation which shall be the basis of recording in the books of accounts the appropriate depreciation expenses pertaining to the current year which should be charged against its appropriate depreciation expense accounts. These are necessary in order to present fairly the PPE accounts with its corresponding Accumulated Depreciation Expense Accounts in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the General Manager direct the Senior Corporate Budget Analyst to locate pertinent records and closely monitor the Construction in progress account thru the maintenance of subsidiary records and other accounting records consistent with the provisions of the MNGAS for LGUs. Likewise, coordinate with the Engineering Division to identify completed projects and request submission of Property Completion and Inventory Report for all completed projects of the Municipality of Tipo-Tipo for proper reclassification and recording to its appropriate asset account, and provision of depreciation for depreciable assets.

We recommended that the officials concerned shall submit the aforementioned reports as required by the above laws and regulations to this Office as well as making available for audit and verification.

Direct the Chief Accountant to: 1. Reconstruct the Subsidiary Ledgers and General Ledgers and General Ledgers of the accounts of the Province to present reliable data on the nature, status and balances of these accounts.
2. Reconcile the Inventory report with the Property cards maintained by the General Service Office and update the Property Ledger Cards in the Accounting Division to present a reliable balance of the PPE of the Municipality.
3. Submit Bank Reconciliation Statement of all accounts.
We recommend that the Municipality of Tipo-tipo adheres strictly to the provision of NGAS Volume II, Section 31, to ensure the reliability of balances appearing in the books.
We recommended the Municipal Engineer to submit monthly monitoring reports for all on-going infrastructure projects in the Municipality.

We recommended that concerned personnel, particularly authorized drivers of government vehicles, to submit regularly to the Office of the Auditor, within ten (10) days of the following month, the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption.
We recommended that the management maintain separate depository account for each fund in their custody or administration in compliance with the law.

We recommended Management to strictly comply with the guidelines of the LBC,
likewise full disclosure policy of the government must be adhered. We recommend
further to submit a written explanation on the above audit observation.

We recommended that the Management strictly adhere with the provision of RA 7160,
RA 9184 and LBC 125 in the preparation and submission of the supplemental budget
and revised annual procurement plan (APP).

We recommended management to comply with Section 3.7 of Local Budget Circular
(LBC) 125, Section 313 of RA 7160 and Section 107 of MNGAS in taking up the
Bayanihan Grant and submit to the Office of the Auditor its subsidiary ledger.

We recommended that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the
Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA
Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for auditor’s immediate review and
verification and to facilitate the immediate preparation of the necessary
correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or errors or other reconciling
items requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct balance of cash accounts in
the financial statements

I. Reconstruct the Subsidiary Ledgers and General Ledgers of the accounts of the
Province to present reliable data on the nature, status and balances of these

We recommended the LCE to instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit monthly
monitoring reports for all on going infrastructure projects in the Municipality.

We recommended that concerned personnel, particularly authorized drivers of
government vehicles, to submit regularly to the Office of the Auditor, within ten (10)
days of the following month, the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption in accordance
with government rules and regulations, attached is the COA Circular 97-61 complete
with sample form of monthly report of fuel consumption. It is then imperative that the
agency should comply on the rules and regulations on fuel consumption and keep its
record in detail, as may be necessary, so that the correct and accurate information will
be provided.

We recommended that the management maintain separate depository account for each
fund in their custody or administration in compliance with the law.

Direct the persons responsible to settle the audit suspension through compliance with
the requirements indicated in the Notice of Suspension (NS) and the persons liable for
the audit disallowance must settle the same in accordance with COA Circular No.
2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, otherwise known as the Rules and Regulations
on the Settlement of Accounts and that COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14,
2012. Re: Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transaction
must be properly observed.

We recommended that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a _ monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for auditor’s immediate review and verification and to facilitate the immediate preparation of the necessary correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or errors or other reconciling items requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct balance of cash accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: “The failure to submit the Bank Reconciliation Statement was due to the Landbank of the Philippines’ delay in releasing the Bank Statement of the Municipality.”

observations.managementAction: “Due to the present situation (Pandemic) majority of the employees are work from home thus resulting to the Municipality’s failure to conduct the inventory count for CY 2020. Rest assured that we will comply with all the audit recommendations given once there are enough manpower and opportunity to conduct the same, following safety guidelines.”

Rest assured that we will comply with all the audit recommendations given once there are enough manpower and opportunity to conduct the same, following safety guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the officials concerned shall submit the aforementioned reports as required by the above laws and regulations to this Office as well as making available for audit, verification.
observations.managementAction: there is a delay on the arrival of said reports at the office of Commission on Audit, Provincial Office, Capitol Building, Isabela City, Basilan. But it be noted that based on the transmittal letter attach with the said reports, no delay was committed by the Municipality.
observations.reason: Due to the present situation (Pandemic) there is a delay on the arrival of said reports at the office of Commission on Audit, Provincial Office, Capitol Building, Isabela City, Basilan. But it be noted that based on the transmittal letter attach with the said reports, no delay was committed by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Chief Accountant to: 1.Reconstruct the Subsidiary Ledgers and General Ledgers of the accounts of the Province to present reliable data on the nature, status and balances of these accounts. 2.Reconcile the Inventory report with the Property cards maintained by the General Service Office and update the Property Ledger Cards in the Accounting Division to present a reliable balance of the PPE of the Municipality. 3.Submit Bank Reconciliation Statement of all accounts.
observations.managementAction: “The Municipality is currently on the process in making all the necessary ledgers, the delay was due to the limited access in the workplace because majority of the employees are work from home. Rest assured that we will do our best to submit the said ledgers as fast as we can.”
observations.reason: The Municipality is currently on the process in making all the necessary ledgers, the delay was due to the limited access in the workplace because majority of the employees are work from home. Rest assured that we will do our best to submit the said ledgers as fast as we can.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management was direct the Accountant to reflect the current portion of Loans Payable separately from its long term debt in the FS, so that its users will be accordingly apprised of the status of its obligation.
We recommend that the Municipality of Tipo-tipo adheres strictly to the provision of NGAS Volume IZ, Section 31, to ensure the reliability of balances appearing in the books.
observations.managementAction: “Appropriate action will be taken to improve the Municipality’s current internal control, particularly in the preparation of the JEV, DVs and Liquidation Reports”.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management maintain separate depository account for each fund in their custody or administration in compliance with the law.
observations.managementAction: “Management assured us that all necessary actions will be complied regarding the opening of a separate depository account for LDRMM, we are just waiting for the approved Resolution by the Sangguniang Bayan authorizing the LCE and the Municipal Treasurer to open an account for the said purpose.”

observations.recommendation: a. Submit the annual local disaster risk reduction fund investment plan (LDRRMFIP) for 2019 DRRM transaction per COA — Circular 2012 - 002 to support the legality of the 2019 DRRM transaction. b. Submit the LDRRMFIP for 2020 to avoid suspending the DRRM 2020 transaction.

a. The Local Treasurer and the Local Accountant to submit to the Local School Board (LSB) of the certified statement containing the following enumerated below, not later than July 15 of the current year as basis for the formulation of SEF budget. 1. Actual SEF income for the immediately preceding fiscal year; II. Actual SEF income for the first two quarters of the current year; III. The estimated SEF income for the last two quarters of the current year; and IV. The annual estimates for the ensuing year. b. The LSB shall furnish the Local Budget Officer, the Local Accountant, and the Local Treasurer a copy of the SEF Budget as basis for disbursement or certification of availability of funds and for recording purposes not later than seven (7) days after the approval of the SEF budget.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to
prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in
accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for auditor’s
immediate review and verification and to facilitate the immediate preparation of
the necessary correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or errors or
other reconciling items requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct
balance of cash accounts in the financial statements.

Recommendations:
We recommended the Management to:
a. Direct the Inventory and Disposal Committee to conduct a complete physical
count inventory, annually, as required; Management should treat the matter of
physical count and appraisal of the PPE as a priority concern. A separate
inventory report shall be prepared for unserviceable PPE which do not
contribute to the economic activity of the Municipality. Derecognize
unserviceable properties in the books and facilitate disposal of these properties
in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445.
b. Intensify efforts to maintain and prepare PPE Ledger cards (PPELC) in
accordance with Section 13, Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume II which
prescribes the maintenance of SL to substantiate the account.
c. The Municipal Accountant and Other Officers concerned should provide for the
depreciation on the depreciable assets. The Property Supply Officer should
ensure that the Property Custodian keeps and maintains updated Property Card
for each PPE account to readily provide pertinent data, thus facilitate the
preparation of the Schedule of Depreciation which shall be the basis of
recording in the books of accounts the appropriate depreciation expenses
pertaining to the current year which should be charged against its appropriate
depreciation expense accounts. These are necessary in order to present fairly
the PPE accounts with its corresponding Accumulated Depreciation Expense
Accounts in the Financial Statements.

We recommend that the General Manager direct the Senior Corporate Budget
Analyst to locate pertinent records and closely monitor the Construction in
progress account thru the maintenance of subsidiary records and other accounting
records consistent with the provisions of the MNGAS for LGUs. Likewise,
coordinate with the Engineering Division to identify completed projects and
request submission of Property Completion and Inventory Report for all
completed projects of the Municipality of Tipo-Tipo for proper reclassification
and recording to its appropriate asset account, and provision of depreciation for
depreciable assets.

We recommend that the officials concerned shall submit the aforementioned
reports as required by the above laws and regulations to this Office as well as making
available for audit verification.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management direct the Accountant to reflect the current
portion of Loans Payable separately from its long term debt in the FS, so that its users
will be accordingly apprised of the status of its obligation.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipality of Tipo-tipo adheres strictly to the provision
of NGAS Volume II, Section 31, to ensure the reliability of balances appearing in the
books.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the management maintain separate depository
account for each fund in their custody or administration in compliance with the law.

Recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRM officer shall
faithfully adhered all the laws and regulation concerning DRRM fund as follows:
a. Submit the annual local disaster risk reduction management fund investment plan
(LDRRMFIP) for 2020 DRRM transaction per COA Circular 2012 - 002 to
support the legality of the 2020 DRRM transaction, and
b. Submit the LDRRMFIP for 2019 to avoid suspending the DRRM 2019
transaction.

Recommendation:
We recommended the following
A. The Local Treasurer and the Local Accountant to submit to the LSB of the
certified statement containing the following enumerated below, not later than July
15 of the current year as basis for the formulation of SEF budget.
I. Actual SEF income for the immediately preceding fiscal year;
II. Actual SEF income for the first two quarters of the current year;
III. The estimated SEF income for the last two quarters of the current
year; and
IV. The annual estimates for the ensuing year.
B. The LSB shall furnish the Local Budget Officer, the Local Accountant, and the
Local Treasurer a copy of the SEF Budget as basis for disbursement or
certification of availability of funds and for recording purposes.

We recommended that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommended the following
A. The Local Treasurer and the Local Accountant to submit to the LSB of the
certified statement containing the following enumerated below, not later than July
15 of the current year as basis for the formulation of SEF budget.
I. Actual SEF income for the immediately preceding fiscal year;
II. Actual SEF income for the first two quarters of the current year;
III. The estimated SEF income for the last two quarters of the current
year; and
IV. The annual estimates for the ensuing year.
B. The LSB shall furnish the Local Budget Officer, the Local Accountant, and the
Local Treasurer a copy of the SEF Budget as basis for disbursement or
certification of availability of funds and for recording purposes not later than
seven (7) days after the approval of the SEF budget.

We recommended that at the end of each year, the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRM officer shall transfer all the unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF - MOOE to the special fund under the account “Trust Liability - DRRM”.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRM officer shall faithfully adhered all the laws and regulation concerning DRRM fund as follows:
a. Submit the annual local disaster risk reduction management fund investment plan (LDRRMFIP) for 2019 DRRM transaction per COA Circular 2012 - 002 to support the legality of the 2019 DRRM transaction, and
b. Submit the LDRRMFIP for 2018 to avoid suspending the DRRM 2018 transaction.

We recommended that at the beginning of each year, the Municipal Engineer submit the list of all on – going government projects/programs/activities (PPA) and those that are to be implemented during the year and submit monthly monitoring PPA report of the agency, as stated in COA Circular 2013 – 004.

We recommended the Municipal Government being primarily responsible for the implementation and enforcement of the provisions of R.A. 9003 within their respective jurisdictions to comply with the laws and regulation stated in Section 12 and Section 16 of the same Act.

We recommended the agency to establish Local Council for the Protection of Children composing of chosen responsible members of the community including a representative from the youth sector, as well as representatives from government and private agencies concerned with the welfare of children.

We recommended the Municipal Government to abide with the provision of R.A. 9344, by appropriating each year in their annual budget one percent of the total IRA for the operation of the LCPC and to maximize the utilization of the fund allocated for the protection of children.

We recommended that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to
prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in
accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for auditor’s
immediate review and verification and to facilitate the immediate preparation of
the necessary correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or errors or
other reconciling items requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct
balance of cash accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended the Management to:
a. Direct the Inventory and Disposal Committee to conduct a complete physical
count inventory, annually, as required; Management should treat the matter of
physical count and appraisal of the PPE as a priority concern. A separate
inventory report shall be prepared for unserviceable PPE which do not
contribute to the economic activity of the Municipality. Derecognize
unserviceable properties in the books and facilitate disposal of these properties
in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445.
b. Intensify efforts to maintain and prepare PPE Ledger cards (PPELC) in
accordance with Section 13, Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume II which
prescribes the maintenance of SL to substantiate the account.
c. The Municipal Accountant and Other Officers concerned should provide for
the depreciation on the depreciable assets. The Property Supply Officer should
ensure that the Property Custodian keeps and maintains updated Property Card
for each PPE account to readily provide pertinent data, thus facilitate the
preparation of the Schedule of Depreciation which shall be the basis of
recording in the books of accounts the appropriate depreciation expenses
pertaining to the current year which should be charged against its appropriate
depreciation expense accounts. These are necessary in order to present fairly
the PPE accounts with its corresponding Accumulated Depreciation Expense
Accounts in the Financial Statements.

We recommend that the General Manager direct the Senior Corporate Budget Analyst
to locate pertinent records and closely monitor the Construction in progress account
thru the maintenance of subsidiary records and other accounting records consistent
with the provisions of the MNGAS for LGUs. Likewise, coordinate with the Engineering Division
to identify completed projects and request submission of Property Completion and
Inventory Report for all completed projects of the Municipality of Tipo-Tipo for proper
reclassification and recording to its appropriate asset account, and provision of
depreciation for depreciable assets.

We recommended that the management maintain separate depository
account for each fund in their custody or administration in compliance with the law.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRM officer shall
faithfully adhered all the laws and regulation concerning DRRM fund as follows:
a. Submit the annual local disaster risk reduction management fund investment plan
(LDRRMFIP) for 2019 DRRM transaction per COA Circular 2012 - 002 to
support the legality of the 2019 DRRM transaction, and
b. Submit the LDRRMFIP for 2018 to avoid suspending the DRRM 2018
transaction.

We recommended the Municipal Government being primarily responsible for the
implementation and enforcement of the provisions of R.A. 9003 within their
respective jurisdictions to comply with the laws and regulation stated in Section 12
and Section 16 of the same Act.

We recommended the agency to establish Local Council for the Protection of
Children composing of chosen responsible members of the community including a
representative from the youth sector, as well as representatives from government and
private agencies concerned with the welfare of children.

Recommendation:
In recognizing the right of children to assistance, including proper care and
nutrition, and special protection from all forms of neglect, abuse, cruelty and
exploitation, and other conditions prejudicial to their development, we recommended
the Municipal Government to abide with the provision of R.A. 9344, by
appropriating each year in their annual budget one percent of the total IRA for the
operation of the LCPC and to maximize the utilization of the fund allocated for the
protection of children.

We recommended that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant will submit the BRS on a screenshot in lieu of the statements when the Landbank of the Philippines provides the complete monthly bank statements or its screenshot.
observations.reason: The Land Bank of the Philippines refuses to provide screenshot in lieu of the statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to:
a. Direct the Inventory and Disposal Committee to conduct a complete physical count inventory, annually, as required; Management should treat the matter of physical count and appraisal of the PPE as a priority concern. A separate inventory report shall be prepared for unserviceable PPE which do not contribute to the economic activity of the Municipality. Derecognize unserviceable properties in the books and facilitate disposal of these properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445.
b. Intensify efforts to maintain and prepare PPE Ledger cards (PPELC) in accordance with Section 13, Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume II which prescribes the maintenance of SL to substantiate the account.
c. The Municipal Accountant and Other Officers concerned should provide for the depreciation on the depreciable assets. The Property Supply Officer should ensure that the Property Custodian keeps and maintains updated Property Card for each PPE account to readily provide pertinent data, thus facilitate the preparation of the Schedule of Depreciation which shall be the basis of recording in the books of accounts the appropriate depreciation expenses pertaining to the current year which should be charged against its appropriate depreciation expense accounts. These are necessary in order to present fairly the PPE accounts with its corresponding Accumulated Depreciation Expense Accounts in the Financial Statements.
We recommended that the management maintain separate depository account for each fund in their custody or administration in compliance with the law.

We recommended the agency to comply with the provision and guidelines stipulated in DILG - DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017 - 001 dated February 22, 2017. The appropriation and utilization of the twenty percent (20%) Development fund shall contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development & environment management outcomes of the LGU and shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditure”

Audited agency to appropriate 5% of the appropriation and shall submit a copy of the Annual GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) to the mother or central offices. Likewise, a copy of the corresponding Accomplishment Report shall be furnished to the said COA Audit Team within five (5) working days from the end of January of the preceding year.

Ensure that identified PPAs address emerging gender-related issues and concerns pursuant to the Gender and Development Plan and Budget of the Municipality and submit monthly accomplishment report to our office.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive in coordination with the Sangguniang Bayan closely monitor the implementation of the projects included in the Annual Investment Plan. Henceforth, take into consideration whether the available manpower has the capability to implement on time the programs, projects and activities to be included in the yearly Annual Investment Plan. Also, we recommend submit to our office the quarterly report on Program/Projects and Activities (PPAs).

Management shall prioritize the implementation of the programs on solid waste and do such other acts required from LGU pursuant to Republic Act 9003 particularly on waste segregation and collection until its disposal to the dumpsite to protect the public from possible health hazard

Recommendation:


            We recommended that  the  head  of  agency  shall  instruct  the
Accountant to    prepare  the  Bank  Reconciliation  Statement  (BRS)  on  a
monthly basis  in        accordance  with  COA  Circular  No.  96-011  dated
October 2, 1996 for auditor’s     immediate  review and verification and  to
facilitate    the    immediate    preparation    of         the    necessary
correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or  errors  or      other
reconciling items requiring corrections in  order  to  reflect  the  correct
balance of cash accounts in the financial statements.

Recommendations:

     a. Direct the Inventory and Disposal Committee to conduct  a  complete
        physical count inventory, annually, as required; Management  should
        treat the matter of physical count and appraisal of the  PPE  as  a
        priority concern. A separate inventory report shall be prepared for

        of the Municipality. Derecognize unserviceable  properties  in  the
        books and facilitate disposal of  these  properties  in  accordance

     b. Intensify efforts to maintain and prepare PPE Ledger cards  (PPELC)
        in accordance with Section 13, Manual on NGAS for LGUs,  Volume  II
        which prescribes the maintenance of SL to substantiate the account.


     c. The  Municipal  Accountant  and  Other  Officers  concerned  should
        provide  for  the  depreciation  on  the  depreciable  assets.  The
        Property Supply Officer should ensure that the  Property  Custodian
        keeps and maintains updated Property Card for each PPE  account  to
        readily provide pertinent data, thus facilitate the preparation  of
        the Schedule of Depreciation which shall be the basis of  recording
        in the books of  accounts  the  appropriate  depreciation  expenses
        pertaining to the current year which should be charged against  its
        appropriate depreciation expense accounts. These are  necessary  in
        order to present fairly the PPE  accounts  with  its  corresponding
        Accumulated  Depreciation  Expense  Accounts   in   the   Financial
        Statements.

Recommendations:

        We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:


         ➢ Adhere to the rules and regulations set forth in R.A. No.  10121
           in setting aside of at least 5% DRRM  fund  from  its  estimated
           revenue from regular sources;


         ➢ Require the LDRRM  Officer  to  submit  the  Monthly  Report  on
           Sources and Utilization of DRRMF within 15th day after  the  end
           of each month.

Recommendation:

            We recommended that the management maintain separate  depository
      account for each fund in their custody or administration in compliance
      with the law.

Recommendation:

            We recommended that at  the  end  of  each  year  the  Municipal
      Accountant  and   the   MDRRM   officer   shall   transfer   all   the
      unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF - MOOE to  the
      special trust fund under the account “Trust Liability - DRRM”.

Recommendation:

            We recommended the Municipal Accountant and  the  MDRRM  officer
      shall faithfully adhered all the laws and regulation  concerning  DRRM
      fund as follows:

       ➢ Submit the annual local disaster  risk  reduction  management  fund
         investment  plan  (LDRRMFIP)   for  2018  DRRM  transaction  per  COA
         Circular 2012 - 002 to  support  the  legality  of  the  2018  DRRM
         transaction, and


       ➢ Submit the LDRRMFIP for 2017 to  avoid  suspending  the  DRRM  2017
         transaction.

Recommendation:

            We recommended at the beginning  of  each  year,  the  Municipal
      Engineer  submit   the   list   of   all   on   –   going   government
      projects/programs/activities and those  that  are  to  be  implemented
      during the year and submit monthly  monitoring  PPA’s  report  of  the
      agency, as stated in COA Circular 2013 – 004.

Recommendation:

             We  recommended  the  Municipal  Government   being   primarily
      responsible for the implementation and enforcement of  the  provisions
      of R.A. 9003 within their respective jurisdictions to comply with  the
      laws and regulation stated in Section 12 and Section 16  of  the  same
      Act.

Recommendation:

            We recommended the agency to establish  Local  Council  for  the
      Protection of Children composing of chosen responsible members of  the
      community including a representative from the youth sector, as well as
      representatives from government and private  agencies  concerned  with
      the welfare of children.

Recommendation:

            In recognizing the right of children  to  assistance,  including
      proper care and nutrition, and special protection from  all  forms  of
      neglect,  abuse,  cruelty  and  exploitation,  and  other   conditions
      prejudicial  to  their  development,  we  recommended  the   Municipal
      Government to abide with the provision of R.A. 9344, by  appropriating
      each year in their annual budget one percent of the total IRA for  the
      operation of the LCPC and to maximize  the  utilization  of  the  fund
      allocated for the protection of children.

Recommendation:

            We recommended the agency  to  comply  with  the  provision  and
      guidelines stipulated in the DILG - DBM Joint Memorandum Circular  No.
      2017 - 001 dated February 22, 2017. The appropriation and  utilization
      of the twenty percent (20%) Development fund shall contribute  to  the
      attainment  of  desirable  socio-economic  development  &  environment
      management outcomes of  the  LGU  and  shall  partake  the  nature  of
      investment or capital expenditure”

Recommendation:

      We recommended the following

     A. The Local Treasurer and the Local Accountant to submit to  the  LSB
        of the certified  statement  containing  the  following  enumerated
        below, not later than July 15 of the current year as basis for  the
        formulation of SEF budget.


            I. Actual SEF income for the immediately preceding fiscal year;
           II. Actual SEF income for the first two quarters of  the  current
               year;
          III. The estimated SEF income for the last  two  quarters  of  the
               current year; and
           IV. The annual estimates for the ensuing year.


     B.  The  LSB  shall  furnish  the  Local  Budget  Officer,  the  Local
        Accountant, and the Local Treasurer a copy of  the  SEF  Budget  as
        basis for disbursement or certification of  availability  of  funds
        and for recording purposes not later than seven (7) days after  the
        approval of the SEF budget.

Recommendation:

            We recommended the Audited agency to appropriate 5% of the total
      budget appropriation of the agency and shall  submit  a  copy  of  the
      Annual GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) to the COA Audit Team assigned to the
      agency within five (5) working days from the received of the  approved
      plan from the PCW or their mother or central offices. Likewise, a copy
      of the corresponding Accomplishment Report shall be furnished the said
      Audit Team within five (5) working days from the end of January of the
      preceding year.

Direct the persons responsible to settle  the  audit  suspension
      through compliance with the requirements indicated in  the  Notice  of
      Suspension (NS) and the persons liable for the audit disallowance must
      settle the same in accordance with COA  Circular  No.  2009-006  dated
      September 15, 2009, otherwise known as the Rules  and  Regulations  on
      the Settlement of Accounts and that COA Circular  No.  2012-001  dated
      June  14,  2012.  Re:  Revised  Documentary  Requirements  for  Common
      Government Transaction must be properly observed.

Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to present reliable data on the nature, status and balances of the accounts. Refer to the available records and files of financial transactions of the agency in order to arrive at a reliable SL balances which correspond to the amount stated in the General Ledger of the major accounts. Reconcile the Inventory Report with the Property Cards maintained by the General Service Office and update the Property Ledger Cards in the Accounting Division to present a reliable balance of the PPE of the Municipality.

The Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer shall maintain 
stock ledger cards, property ;edger cards, WOABSLC and 
other subsidiary records and reconcile accounting and 
property records periodically;
The Inventory Committee shall conduct complete physical 
inventory of all property and reconcile results with 
accounting records and record any adjustments in the 
books;
The Municipal Accountant shall provide depreciation on all 
depreciable assets and ensure the reconciliation and 
update of the aforementioned dormant accounts; and
The Municipal Treasurer/Property officer should prepare 
the Inventory and Inspection Report of all identified 
unserviceable properties and facilitate their immediate 
disposal to prevent further deterioration and decrease in 
Salvage values. The Municipal Accountant should effect 
the necessary adjustments in the PPE Account to 
reclassify the book value of unserviceable properties to 
Other Assets account pending disposal and close the 
Corresponding Accumulated Depreciation.

Maximize the application of the LGU’s power to levy real 
property tax pursuant to Sections 232 and 233, Chapter 4 
of the Local Government Code or R.A. 7160.

Impose Special Levy on Real Property in accordance with Section 235 of Chapter 5, R.A. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991 which should be 1% on the assessed value of real property, which is the same with the basic tax

The accountable officers/collectors should remit/turnover their collections to the liquidating officer for deposit to the depository bank on a regular basis in accordance with Section 29 of the NGAS Manual. The Municipal Treasurer should set more effective control measures to avoid or minimized delayed remittance of collections and institute disciplinary action on concerned accountable employees under her/his supervision, if necessary.

Instruct the HRMO-designate to ensure the preparation of the HR Plan composed of those enumerated under CSC MC No. 3, series of 2012; and 
Ensure that duties and responsibilities are specifically stipulated in all contract of services/MOA or Job Order Contracts, Specific instructions  given by those competent authorities to which specific employees are detailed which  should be likewise attached to the contract

Direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management council to strictly comply with the provisions of the Joint Memorandum Circular that only allowable expenses are charges to the fund; and
Direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management council to prepare the Report of Sources and Utilization of DRRMF monthly duly certified by the Local Accountant and submits same to the Office of the Auditor.

The Municipal Accountant should set aside as Subsidy to Other Funds and transfer prior years unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF from the General Fund to the Trust Fund books using the “Trust Liability-DRRM” (code 438) Subsidiary Ledger for “Trust Liability=DRRM” (Code 438).
Subsidiary Ledger for “Trust Liability-DRRM” (code 438) must be maintained.
The Municipal Budget Officer maintains record of the appropriation, corresponding expenditures and balances of the LDRRMF.

Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office to 
promptly submit the required Quarterly Report on 
Government Projects/Programs/Activities.

Likewise, we also recommend that the details required in 
the Quarterly Report on Government 
Projects/Programs/Activities should be properly filled-in

We recommended that in the ensuing years, the Local Chief Executive should instruct the Municipal Budget Office to immediately submit to the SP the action taken on the findings and recommendations observed by the LFC so that the appropriation ordinance shall be declared operative its entirety.

We recommended that the Local School Board should submit to agency concerned the School Board Budget to evaluate the programs/projects to be included in the SEF Budget taking into consideration the objectives set forth under existing laws and regulations that govern the utilization of such fund.

The Accountant and the Property Officer should require the end users of the gasoline, oil and lubricants to prepare and submit duly accomplished the driver’s trip ticket, report of official travel and monthly report of fuel consumption for verification to the reasonableness of fuel consumption.

The concerned officer/staff should remit the GSIS contributions/premiums as soon as possible and comply with the GSIS regulation.  The remittances should take priority over the payment of any and all other obligations, except salaries and wages of its employees; and
We  are also requesting the Accountant    you to furnish our office a machine copy of the Memorandum of Agreement entered into by your municipality and the GSIS for the purpose of full disclosure and record
The responsible officials should see to it that withholding taxes on compensation are remitted on time, in accordance with BIR regulations

The Municipal Accountant should coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to identify the employees with unremitted contributions to enable the Municipal Government to settle its obligations. In coordination with the Human Resource Officer, the LGU should devise a system to facilitate the preparation of the list of employees for the remittance of contributions to the Pag-IBIG within the prescribed period. (See Annex-C for schedule)
Require the person/unit concerned to strictly comply with Section 20 of the IRR of RA No. 7875. (See Annex-D for schedule)

Properly  Observe COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 the prescribed  deadline for the submission of the receipt and disbursement records with all paid vouchers, official receipts, reports and supporting documents within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month.

Ensure that identified PPAs address emerging gender-
related issues and concerns pursuant to PCW DILG DNM 
and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01;
Utilization of GAD Budget Should be in accordance with 
the DILG-approved GAD Plan and Budget; and
Submit monthly accomplishment report to our office.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive in coordination with the Sangguaniang Bayan closely monitor the implementation of the projects included in the Annual Investment Plan.  Henceforth, take into consideration whether the available manpower has the capability to implement on time the programs, projects and activities to be included in the yearly Annual Investment Plan.
Also, we recommend submit to our office the quarterly report on Program/Projects and Activities (PPAs).

Direct the persons responsible to settle the audit suspension through compliance with the requirements indicated in the Notice of Suspension (NS) and the persons liable for the audit disallowance must settle the same in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, otherwise known as the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts and that COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Re: Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transaction must be properly observed.

We recommend that all accountable officers prepare and submit the RAAF to the Office of the Treasurer at the end of each month to ensure all accountable forms issued are properly recorded and reported. Also, we recommend that the Office of the Municipal Treasurer prepare and submit the CRAAF to the Office of the Auditor as required under Section 51 of the NGAS, Volume II, for LGUs.

The concerned officer/staff should maintain separate special accounts in the General Fund for the Market Operations and Slaughterhouse Operations with the respective set of subsidiary ledgers likewise, the post-closing trial balance and related financial statements for each special account at the end of the year should be prepared.

The concerned officer/staff should remit the GSIS contributions/premiums as soon as possible and comply with the GSIS regulation. The remittances should take priority over the payment of any and all other obligations, except salaries and wages of its employees. We also requesting you to furnish our office a machine copy of the Memorandum of Agreement entered into by your municipality and the GSIS for the purpose of full disclosure and record.
The responsible officials should see to it that withholding taxes on compensation are remitted on time, in accordance with BIR regulations.

The Municipal Accountant should coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to identify the employees with unremitted contributions to enable the Municipal Government to settle its obligations. In coordination with the Human Resource Officer, the LGU should devise a system to facilitate the preparation of the list of employees for the remittance of contributions to the Pag-IBIG within the prescribed period.
Require the person/unit concerned to strictly comply with Section 20 of the IRR of RA No. 7875.

Together with the bidding, BAC resolution minutes of the proceedings, abstracts, and such other documents peculiar to the contract and/or to the mode of procurement which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.

We recommend that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996. The monthly BRS from January-November, 2012 shall be submitted immediately to the auditor for review and verification. We further recommend that the management shall re-evaluate their internal control on accounting and financial operations to prevent possible loss of government funds due to not PPSAS compliance.

The Municipal Accountant should set aside as Subsidy to Other Funds and transfer prior years unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF from the General Fund to the Trust Fund books using the “Trust Liability-DRRM” (code 438) Subsidiary Ledger for “Trust Liability=DRRM” (Code 438). Subsidiary Ledger for “Trust Liability-DRRM” (code 438) be maintained. The Municipal Budget Officer maintain record of the appropriation, corresponding expenditures and balances of the LDRRMF.

Direct the persons responsible to settle the audit suspension through compliance with the requirements indicated in the Notice of Suspension (NS) and the persons liable for the audit disallowance must settle the same in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, otherwise known as the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts and that COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Re: Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transaction must be properly observed.
